mix.note: Base plus a utilization/quota-adjacent bonus is the norm across this data (Starburst's Onboarding Engineer posting explicitly notes '+ commission or + bonus'; Sitreps' Implementation Engineer posting cites a 15-30% bonus; Corelight and Okta postings reference commission/OTE structures). No posting in the validated USD-base set discloses an exact numeric base/bonus split, so 75%/25% is an industry-typical estimate, not a figure directly observed in this data. Equity is mentioned at vendor in-house PS orgs (Adyen, Pendo, GitLab, Samsara, Veeam) but rarely at pure consultancies/SIs (Reply, GDIT, VTS) or telecom (AT&T, Cisco), consistent with base + bonus with little/no equity at the latter.

caveat: Entry/Associate rests on 4 companies (Reply, Greenlight Guru, Aspire, VTS) and Principal/Director+ on 2 (AT&T, Veeam Software) — both edge bands are thinner than the Mid/Senior core. Because entry-level and director-level professional-services/consulting-engineer roles are posted far less frequently than mid-level implementation-consultant roles industry-wide, 3-4 verified companies per edge band is close to the practical ceiling for this archetype without relying on unverifiable aggregator averages.
The Principal/Director+ band rests on only 2 companies (CoreWeave, Salesforce), thinner than the other bands in this archetype, so it should be read as directional rather than precise.

Seniority is inferred from title keywords, not verified level: the "Mid (unspecified level)" bucket includes some senior-leaning titles (ClickHouse's 'Technical Account Manager Architect', several Hightouch 'Enterprise' TAM postings) whose upper end ($200K-$221K) nearly matches the Senior/Staff ceiling, so the Mid/Senior split should be read as directional rather than a clean ladder. Sample is 23 distinct USD-base companies, just under the 25-company bar for high confidence.

levels.label: Mid (unspecified level)
companies: GDIT (General Dynamics Information Technology, subsidiary of General Dynamics)

levels.label: Principal/Director+ (Manager/VP)
companies: GDIT (General Dynamics Information Technology, subsidiary of General Dynamics)

caveat: Entry/Associate rests on a single Google L3 data point ($145K base / $188K total comp per Levels.fyi, crowdsourced not employer-published). This looks like a structural feature of the archetype: DevRel/advocacy roles almost universally require 2+ years of prior software-engineering or community experience as a prerequisite, so companies rarely post a true entry-level requisition for this title. The Trunk.io Senior Developer Advocate figure ($140K-$190K, 0.1%-0.3% equity) is corroborated by three independently-indexed sources showing identical numbers.

Anduril is the single largest contributor to this archetype's data by a wide margin — more than 3x the next-largest employer (Boeing) — so its standardized per-level pay bands ($166K-$220K Mid, $165K-$292K Senior/Staff) meaningfully shape the interior of both bands, even though the low/high extremes are set by other companies (Boeing floor, Neuralink/Roku/Aurora Innovation ceiling).

Engineering Management is overwhelmingly salaried rather than commission-based, even though several source postings (Anthropic, Betterment, Verkada, Klaviyo, Harvey, Amplitude, and others) carry generic 'on-target-earnings/commission if applicable' boilerplate copied from a company's sales-role comp policy — that language is treated as base pay here, not variable comp. A handful of Anthropic postings ($405K-$850K, frontier-AI-lab comp) sit well above the rest of the market and drive the top of the Manager band; a non-representative $850,000 outlier cluster is excluded from that ceiling.

Entry/Associate is backed by a single company (Five9); the only additional entry-level figures available (Glassdoor/ZipRecruiter/Salary.com/Levels.fyi averages) are crowdsourced or blended total-comp numbers from a fundamentally different measurement methodology than the posted-range base figures used elsewhere in this archetype, so they aren't merged in — doing so would silently change what the number represents. This band should be read as directional. Principal/Director+ rests on 4 companies, including Huntress's "Director, Sales Engineering, MSP" posting ($250,000–$272,000 OTE, 80/20 base/OTE split → ~$200,000–$217,600 base). It's still a thin band by company count and should be read with some caution.

mix.note: Derived from Fivetran, Orca Security, and Braze paired base/OTE postings (~75% base / 25% variable). Huntress's data point (80/20 base/OTE split, i.e. 80% base) is directionally consistent with this estimate but wasn't used to recompute it, since it's a single data point for a Director-level role, not representative of the archetype as a whole.

levels.label: Principal/Director+ (Manager/VP)
companies: GDIT (General Dynamics Information Technology, subsidiary of General Dynamics)

caveat: Entry/Associate rests on 2 companies (Boeing; Capital One's 'Cyber Security Development Program Associate - 2026,' a rotational-program posting disclosing $123,000 base in Plano, TX and $135,000 base in McLean, VA, plus eligibility for performance-based cash bonus/LTI), thinner than every other band in this archetype (9-91 companies each), so it should be read as directional. Most employers hire security engineers laterally or route new grads through generalist SWE/analyst pipelines rather than posting distinct 'Entry/Associate Security Engineer' requisitions, so the addressable public posting pool for this slice is inherently small — a pattern also seen in customer-support-engineer and sre-production-engineer.

GDIT accounts for roughly 37% of raw extracted data points, meaningfully over-represented relative to commercial competitors like Accenture, Deloitte, Cognizant, Slalom, West Monroe, EPAM, and Publicis Sapient, because GDIT's Workday postings publish pay ranges on nearly every req while most commercial SI/consultancy postings for this title don't disclose salary outside pay-transparency states (CA/CO/NY/WA). Six companies' contributions (Deloitte, Accenture, Cognizant, IBM, Capgemini, Slalom) are base-dominated but not always literally base-only — a handful carry a small real variable-comp component (roughly 5-13% of total), so those figures modestly overstate true base pay and should be read as a base-pay proxy accurate to within roughly 10%, not an exact base figure.

Entry/Associate and Principal/Director+ each rest on only 3 companies. Entry/Associate includes a posted range for Amazon (Associate Solutions Architect, 2026 early-career cohort, $98,200-$160,000, Austin TX/Arlington VA/Seattle WA), while Salesforce's and Snowflake's entry-level numbers rest on Levels.fyi/Glassdoor aggregate data rather than a live posting — neither appears to publish base-salary ranges on its Solutions Architect postings. Principal/Director+ combines Cloudflare ('Principal Solutions Architect - Cloudflare One,' $248,000-$303,000 in Chicago/Atlanta rising to $257,000-$314,000 in NYC/DC/Seattle/LA), Datadog, and Amazon (Levels.fyi-derived). Remote.com's Senior Partner Solutions Architect posting ($53,850-$136,300) is excluded from the Senior/Staff band: its range is a blended multi-country ('AMER timezones') band, not a US-comparable figure.

Entry/Associate is a real, citable band built from two live job postings: TikTok's "Software Engineer Graduate (Site Reliability Engineer) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)" in San Jose ($122,574-$187,200 base, no prior-experience requirement) and SWIFT's "Associate Site Reliability Engineer - Hybrid" in Culpeper, VA ($64,282-$119,382, 0-2 years experience). True entry-level SRE hiring is rare and concentrated in two patterns invisible to a plain "SRE" title search — big-tech "New Grad/Graduate SRE" university-hire pipelines (TikTok) and "Associate SRE" titles at large non-tech/financial-infrastructure operators (SWIFT). Because this band rests on only 2 companies, and Principal/Director+ on only 3 (AT&T, Disney, Morgan Stanley), both should be read as directional, not precise.

Mid and Principal/Director+ each rest on 4 source companies, well short of Senior/Staff's 14. The thinness looks like a genuine market-supply limit rather than a sourcing gap: most employers posting a 'Technical Product Manager' title skip straight to Senior/Staff-equivalent scope (e.g. Wheelhouse's plain 'Technical Product Manager' posting still requires 5+ years PM experience), and true Director+-level TPM postings with disclosed pay are rare. Treat the Mid and Principal/Director+ bands as directional, not tightly bounded.
